text: CIDRAP Op-Ed: Why the FDA tried to bury studies showing vaccines are safe The COVID and shingles vaccine studies were suppressed because they showed the vaccine-safety system works.

Report: Severe COVID can catalyze arterial plaque growth, sparking deadly heart problems Plaque growth can lead to a higher risk of heart attack, stroke, and other life-threatening cardiovascular events for as long as 1 year. Study shows for most, long-COVID symptoms persist into second year Post-exertional malaise, or exercise intolerance, was seen in 36% of those with long COVID.

Lawmakers ask Kennedy about blocked COVID vaccine study Quick takes: BioNTech closures; Texas alert on New World screwworm; second Saluda County, SC, measles case Study: Universal COVID screening among hospitalized patients has unintended consequences Moderna’s combo flu-COVID vaccine receives European authorization Six symptoms/conditions more common in long COVID, suggesting overlap with other post-viral infections Get weekly COVD-19 updates in your inbox.

New research chips away at COVID-19 blood clot mystery Patients are at higher risk of heart attack and stroke if COVID spreads to the bloodstream, according to a new study. Combination of COVID, severe heart attack confers 25% greater risk of death, data suggest The death rate was more than double that before the pandemic.

Air pollution linked to recurrent respiratory infections in infants New data show a ‘clear and significant’ association between ambient air quality and respiratory issues in the first year of life. The State of US Vaccine Policy — Apr 28, 2026 We recap last week's multiple RFK Jr testimonies, some potentially good news at the federal level, and state vaccine policy news.

Izzy Brandstetter Figueroa, MPH, Jess Steier, DrPH COVID virus clears from placenta weeks after maternal infection, study suggests As early as 40 days after infection, no trace of SARS-CoV-2 was found in placentas from women who recovered from COVID-19.

Paxlovid doesn’t reduce hospitalization, death rates in vaccinated high-risk COVID outpatients, trial shows But it does reduce viral load and speed time to recovery. CDC blocks publication of report showing COVID vaccine efficacy The report was scheduled to be published in the CDC’s flagship publication on March 19.

US states took divergent actions in absence of federal COVID vaccine guidance last respiratory virus season The measures may have long-term implications for future vaccine policy, the authors say. COVID vaccines tied to less hospital care, long COVID, and economic burden A large study in Germany shows lower hospitalization, all-cause death rates, and health care costs in vaccinated people.

Study links long COVID in kids to worse grades, attention, and social life School-aged children with long COVID had more than double the risk of decreased academic performance than those without the condition. Unrestricted financial support provided by.
